title: rudbeckia triloba var. rupestris
intro: Rudbeckia triloba var. rupestris, also known as rocky coneflower, is a variety of flowering plant in the daisy family native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The medicinal properties of Rudbeckia triloba var. rupestris are said to offer various health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, and exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant effects. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions to support the immune system, reduce fever, and promote overall well-being.
